criteriaResults.evidence: python -c 'from harness.lib import git' exits 0; module imports cleanly with stdlib-only deps.
All 9 functions resolved as callables: create_harness_branch, create_sprint_branch, merge_sprint, fail_sprint_attempt, commit_harness_state, create_pr, create_fix_pr, create_issue, generate_pr_body.
test_creates_branch passed: returns 'harness/test-project' and current branch matches. git.py:104 sets harness_branch = f'harness/{project_slug}'.
test_idempotent passed: second call after checkout main does not raise; git.py:118-119 falls back to checkout when create -b fails.
test_creates_sprint_branch passed: returns 'harness/test-project-sprint-01' (zero-padded) at git.py:131.
test_cleans_up_existing_branch passed: stale old.txt removed; git.py:135-136 force-deletes existing branch before recreating from harness branch.

test_creates_merge_commit passed: 40-char hex SHA, current branch back on harness, merge message exactly 'harness(sprint-01): merge (PASS, attempt 1)'. git.py:176-181 uses --no-ff with canonical message.
test_creates_tag passed: tag 'harness/sprint-01/pass' present after merge. git.py:185-186.
test_deletes_sprint_branch passed: sprint branch no longer exists post-merge. git.py:189.
test_tags_the_attempt passed: 'harness/sprint-01/attempt-1' present in tag list. git.py:203-204.
test_deletes_sprint_branch and test_returns_to_harness_branch both passed. git.py:209,212 check out harness branch and force-delete sprint branch.
test_handles_dirty_working_tree passed: git stash -q + checkout + stash drop -q sequence at git.py:208-210 mirrors bash behaviour.
test_commits_changes passed: HEAD commit message contains 'harness: test commit'. git.py:222-229 stages harness-state and commits with the supplied message.
test_noop_when_clean passed: HEAD SHA unchanged after invocation on clean repo. git.py:225-227 short-circuits via diff --cached --quiet.
test_contains_sprint_table passed: generated body contains 'Sprint' header and 'PASS' status from fixture eval-report. generate_pr_body at git.py:388-470.
test_sprint_number_zero_padded_two_digits and test_padding_in_tag_for_single_digit_sprint both passed: 'harness/p-sprint-09' branch and 'harness/sprint-09/pass' tag use sprint_pad consistently.
All three TestGracefulDegradation tests passed. git.py:238-247, 283-292, 354-360 check shutil.which('gh') and origin remote, log warnings, and return without raising. create_issue returns ''.

tests/layer1/test_git.py defines 19 test_* methods spanning 7 TestCase classes; covers all 13 bats scenarios (create-branch, idempotent, sprint-branch-creates, sprint-branch-cleanup, merge-creates-merge-commit, merge-creates-tag, merge-deletes-branch, fail-tags-attempt, fail-deletes-branch, fail-returns-to-harness, commit-state-commits, commit-state-noop-when-clean, generate-pr-body-contains-table) plus padding (C3-16), dirty-tree (C3-12), and graceful-degradation (C3-17) extras.
python tests/run-all.py layer1 ran 77 tests in 23.774s with OK status (zero failures, zero errors).

test_git.py imports HarnessTestCase from tests.helpers.test_helper at line 22; every TestCase subclass calls self.init_test_repo() in setUp (lines 62, 90, 132, 192, 246, 274, 310). Tests run in isolated temp git repos.
python -m unittest tests.layer1.test_utils tests.layer1.test_invoke ran 58 tests with OK status. No regressions in Sprint 1 or Sprint 2 functionality.
git diff harness/sprint-02/pass -- '*.sh' '*.ps1' produced empty output. No .sh or .ps1 file modified by this sprint.
AST-parsed imports in git.py: __future__, json, os, shutil, subprocess, pathlib, typing, and local harness.lib.utils. All stdlib + local utils; no third-party packages.

regressionResults.evidence: tests.layer1.test_utils (35 tests) and tests.layer1.test_invoke (22 tests) all pass. Combined run: 58 tests OK in 0.978s.

holisticScores.notes: Faithful port of bash git.sh: branch/tag names match byte-for-byte, merge commit messages exact, graceful gh/origin fallback well-handled. _run/_git_ok/_git_out helpers cleanly encapsulate subprocess details. Module-level docstring documents naming conventions. Stdlib only as required.

recommendations: Optional: typing.Optional is imported but unused in git.py:23 -- safe to remove on a future cleanup pass.
Optional: in generate_pr_body, the dual-path sprint_count fallback (json_read -> json.load) is a safety belt; could be simplified to a single direct json.load now that fixtures are stable.
